title: chaerophyllum colensoi var. multifidum
intro: Chaerophyllum colensoi var. multifidum, also known as New Zealand parsley, is a perennial plant in the carrot family native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in vitamins and minerals, and consuming it is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Chaerophyllum colensoi var. multifidum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional cooking and as a garnish.
